0.text: TCD 001/2023 Bond Interior Design LLC v Eleveight Restaurant & Entertainment Center LLC

1.text: SEPTEMBER 01, 2023 TECHNOLOGY AND CONSTRUCTION DIVISION - ORDERS

3.text: THE DUBAI INTERNATIONAL FINANCIAL CENTRE COURTS

4.text: IN THE COURT OF FIRST INSTANCE

11.text: CASE MANAGEMENT ORDER OF H.E. JUSTICE MAHA AL MHEIRI

12.text: UPON reviewing the Court fi le

13.text: AND UPON reading the Case Management Bundle

14.text: AND UPON reviewing the Rules of the DIFC Courts (the “RDC”)

15.text: AND UPON hearing Counsel for the Claimant and Counsel for the Defendant at the Case Management Conference held before me on 17 August 2023

16.text: AND UPON the Claimant and the Defendant having agreed to the terms of this Order

19.text: 1. Adjacent to each paragraph of each witness statement, reply witness statement (if any) and skeleton argument shall be inserted the issue or issues to which that paragraph relates as numbered in the Agreed List of Issues, in order for the Court to understand to which of the agreed issues that paragraph relates.

20.text: List of Issues for the Claimant’s Claim1

21.text: 2. What is the proper construction of the Contracts?

22.text: 3. Whose responsibility was it to provide authority approvals?

23.text: 4. Who delayed in providing instructions, drawings, authority approvals, site clearances or appointing specialised suppliers or contractors?

24.text: 5. Did the Claimant comply with the notice requirements contained in the Contracts?

25.text: 6. Did the Claimant comply with the requirements and specifi cations of the Project outlined in the Contracts?

26.text: 7. What variations were approved and granted pursuant to the terms of those Contracts?

27.text: 8. Whether the Defendants complied with the requirements to notify any objections to notices issued by the Claimant.

28.text: 9. Whether the Defendant complied with the obligation to make payments to the Claimants under the Contracts.

29.text: 10. Whether the Defendant unreasonably refused to issue a Taking Over Certifi cate to the Claimant in relation to the works completed.

30.text: 11. If the Defendant is found to have breached the Contracts, is the Claimant entitled to damages or compensation?

31.text: 12. If the Claimant is entitled to compensation, what is the amount of those damages or that compensation?

32.text: 13. If the Claimant is entitled to compensation, what interest rate/s should apply to that compensation?

33.text: 14. Whether the Claimant delay the Project completion.

34.text: 15. Whether the Claimant failed to fi nish the Project on time, as outlined in the contracts.

35.text: 16. Whether the Claimant provided low quality work.

36.text: 17. Whether the Claimant failed to make suffi cient manpower available to maintain productivity.

37.text: 18. If the Claimant is found to have breached the contracts, is the Defendant entitled to damages or compensation?

38.text: 19. If the Defendant is entitled to compensation, what is the amount of those damages or compensation?

39.text: 20. If the Defendant is entitled to compensation, what interest rate/s should apply to that compensation?

41.text: 21. The replacement of “What is the correct amounts for the fi nal account for the Project.” with Provided that there are neither defects nor unjustifi ed delays in the execution of works, what is a fair amount to be paid as fi nal account (if any) of the project?

43.text: 22. Standard production of documents shall be made by each party by no later than 4pm on 8 September 2023.

44.text: 23. The parties shall fi le and serve a Request to Produce, if any, by no later than 4pm on 20 September 2023.

45.text: 24. Where there are no objections to a particular Request contained in a Request to Produce, documents responsive to that request shall be produced within 10 days from the date of the Request to Produce, and in any event by no later than 4pm on 30 September 2023.

46.text: 25. Objections to Requests to Produce, if any, may be fi led and served by no later than 4pm on 27 September 2023.

47.text: 26. If a party is not satisfi ed with the objections to any Requests to Produce it may apply to the Court for a Document Production Order immediately using the Part 23 Form (the “Document Production Application”). The usual timelines under RDC 23, for progression of such an application, will apply.

48.text: 27. Once the Document Production Application is fully progressed, the matter will be put before the Court for determination.

49.text: 28. The parties shall comply with the terms of any Disclosure Order and fi le a Document Production Statement within 14 days from the date of the Order.

51.text: 29. Signed statements of witnesses of fact, and hearsay notices where required by the RDC shall be exchanged 4 weeks following the close of the disclosure stage, and in any event by no later than 4pm on 25 October 2023.

52.text: 30. Any Witness Statement evidence in reply shall be fi led and served within 2 weeks thereafter and in any event by no later than 4pm on 15 November 2023.

53.text: 31. Unless otherwise ordered, Witness Statements shall stand as evidence in chief of the witness at trial.

55.text: 32. The Claimant shall fi le and serve any Expert Report(s) within 2 weeks of the close of witness evidence, and in any event by no later than 4pm on 29 November 2023 in respect of the following issues:

57.text: 33. The Defendant shall fi le and serve any Expert Report(s) in respect of those same issues within 2 weeks thereafter, and in any event by no later than 4pm on 13 December 2023.

58.text: 34. The DIFC Courts shall, at the Progress Monitoring Date, consider what directions to give concerning a meeting and discussion between experts.

60.text: 35. A pre-trial review shall be listed on 11 January 2024, being within the normal range of 4 to 8 weeks before trial.

62.text: 36. Agreed trial bundles shall be fi led and served no later than 2 weeks before trial and in any event by no later than 4pm on 29 January 2024.

63.text: Reading List and Trial Timetable (RDC Part 35)

64.text: 37. An agreed reading list for trial along with an estimate of time required for reading and an estimated timetable for trial shall be fi led with the Court by the Claimant no later than two clear days before trial and in any event by no later than 4pm on 7 February 2024.

66.text: 38. Skeleton Arguments and Written Opening Statements shall be fi led and served two clear days before the start of trial for the Claimant and in any event by no later than 4pm on 6 February 2024 and one clear day before the start of trial for the Defendant and in any event by no later than 4pm on 8

68.text: 39. The parties shall prepare an agreed Chronology of signifi cant events cross-referenced to signifi cant documents, pleadings and witness statements which shall be fi led with the Court by the Claimant by no later than 4pm on 9 February 2024. In the event that there are areas of disagreement, the Chronology shall include an agreed Chronology and a Chronology of events which are disputed, with the parties’ respective positions outlined therein.

70.text: 40. The trial of this matter shall be listed on 12 February 2024 with an estimated duration of 5 days.

71.text: 41. Costs of the Case Management Conference shall be costs in the case.

72.text: 42. The parties shall have liberty to apply.

Delvin Sumo

Date of issue: 1 September 2023   
At: 3:30pm
